items.name: Endotracheal Tube Depth
information: ● If IBW calculated, ETT depth based on patient height
● appropriate endotracheal tube depth should be confirmed by Chest X-Ray as soon as feasible.

items.name: Premedications
notes: Minimal supportive evidence the premedications improve outcomes in intubation

items.name: Induction Medications
notes: Consider decreasing the induction dose in hypotensive patients

items.name: Ketamine IV/IO
information: ● Preferred if bronchospasm present, evidence now shows it is safe in head injured patients
● Standard dosing 1-2 mg/kg. Some recommendations for lower dosing in hypotensive patients to 0.75 mg/kg
● To decrease risk of respiratory depression and apnea, administer IV bolus doses over >30 to 60 seconds

items.name: Ketamine IM
information: Can use with Succinylcholine IM if no IV access

items.name: Midazolam IV/IO
information: Risk of decreased BP, HR, RR

items.name: Propofol IV/IO
information: Risk of decreased BP, HR, RR

items.name: Paralytics
notes: ● Considering increasing paralytics doses in hypotensive patients.
● Succinylcholine and Rocuronium are preferred for RSI due to rate of onset, shorter duration.

items.name: Rocuronium IV/IO
information: ● Standard dose is 1-1.2 mg/kg dose preferred for RSI
● Some recommendations for increased dose to 2 mg/kg in hypotensive patients

items.name: Succinylcholine IV/IO
information: Avoid in renal failure, suspected hyperkalemia

items.name: Succinylcholine IM
information: Use with Ketamine IM if no IV access
Avoid in renal failure, suspected hyperkalemia

items.name: Pancuronium IV/IO
information: No longer recommended for use in RSI

items.name: Sugammadex IV/IO
information: for emergent rocuronium and vecuronium reversal
caution in patients with cardiac disease
following sugammadex use, if immediate neuromuscular blockade is needed, use cisatracurium or atracurium.

dosages.label: ● Immediate Rocuronium Reversal (not validated for vecuronium)

items.name: Fentanyl Infusion
information: ● Concentrations very based on each pharmacy, confirm concentration before initiating any infusions.
● Start at 25 mcg/hr, titrate by 10 mcg/hr up to 300 mcg/hr

items.name: Hydromorphone Infusion
information: ● Start at 0.25 mg/hr titrate by 0.25-0.5 mg/hr up to 4 mg/hr
● Concentrations very for each pharmacy, confirm concentration before initiating any infusions.

items.name: Propofol Infusion
information: ● titrate by 5-20 mcg/kg/min Q 5-10 minutes to sedation goal

items.name: Ketamine Infusion
information: ● 5 mcg/kg/min, titrate by 2.5 mcg/kg/min Q5 min

items.name: Dexmedetomidine  IV/IO Bolus
information: 100 mcg/mL concentrated solutions requires dilution before administration

items.name: Dexmedetomidine Infusion
information: usually diluted in solution to a 4 mcg/mL concentration
titrate by 0.2 mcg/kg/hr every 30 mins to sedation goal

items.name: Vecuronium Infusion
information: ● 0.8-1.7 mcg//kg/min, titrate by 0.1 mcg/kg/min Q15 min per ventilator synchrony or TOF

items.name: Rocuronium Infusion
information: ● 3-16 mcg//kg/min, titrate by 0.5-1 mcg/kg/min Q60 min per ventilator synchrony or TOF

items.name: Cisatracurium Infusion
information: ● 1 to 3 mcg/kg/minute (or 0.06 to 0.18 mg/kg/hour); adjust rate accordingly

items.name: Ventilator Settings
notes: ● Ventilator settings should be initiated based on patient's primary medical condition
● TV should be based on patient's Ideal Body Weight (IBW)

items.name: Mode
information: ● SIMV: with obstructive airway disease and intact respiratory drive (asthma, some COPD)
● Pressure Control: intact respiratory effort and non-severe respiratory failure

items.name: Tidal Volume
information: ● adjust to plateau pressure < 35 cm H2O

items.name: Rate
information: Adjust based on EtCO2 and ph levels

items.name: FiO2
information: ● initiate at 100% and ween down as tolerated

items.name: Avoid Peak Pressures > 40
information: ● Adjust to plateau pressure < 35 cm H2O

steps.algostep: Agressive, resists care, pulls at IV / tubes
Anxious, apprehensive, not aggressive or vigorous
not fully alert, sustained awakening to voice
Movement of eye-opening to voice (no eye contact)
No response to voice, movement or eye opening to physical stimulation
No response to voice or physical stimulation

items.name: Agitation
notes: Disclaimer:
● All patients receiving chemical restraints / sedation should be closely monitored and frequently reassessed due to risk of excessive sedation and need for ventilatory support.
● Anti-psychotics have risk of QT prolongation and subsequent cardiac arrhythmia.
● Chemical restraints should only be used when verbal deescalation techniques have failed and the patient's behavior puts them or others at risk for harm.

items.name: Lorazepam IV/IM
information: ● Can be given more frequently if severe agitation

items.name: Midazolam IV/IM
information: ● faster onset / shorter duration than Lorazepam

items.name: Haloperidol IV/IM
information: ● (not currently FDA approved for IV use)

items.name: Diphenhydramine IM/IV
information: ● used to prevent or treat acute dystonic reactions associated with anti-psychotic agents

items.name: Benzotropine IM/IV
information: ● used to prevent or treat acute dystonic reactions associated with anti-psychotic agents

items.name: Ketamine IM
information: ● 4 mg/kg IM- use ideal or adjusted body weight in obesity. Consider max of 250 or 500mg in extreme agitation.

items.name: Ketamine IV
information: 0.5-1 mg/kg IV- use ideal or adjusted body weight in obesity. Consider max of 50 or 100mg in extreme agitation
● To decrease risk of respiratory depression and apnea, administer IV bolus doses over >30 to 60 seconds

items.name: Olanzapine IV/IM
information: IM preferred; if given IV monitor closely for respiratory depression
IV: 5-10 mg, repeat dose as needed after 20 mins (max dose: 20 mg/day)
IM: 5-10 mg Q2 hours (max of 30 mg/day)

items.name: Olanzapine PO
information: 5-10 mg PO, repeat dose as needed after 2 hours (max dose: 20 mg/day)

items.name: Ziprasidone IM
information: 10 mg Q2 hours or 20 mg Q4 hours (max of 40 mg/day)

steps.algostep:
● Benadryl 50 mg IM
● Haloperidol 5 mg IM
● Lorazepam 2 mg IM

● Droperidol 5 mg IM
● Midazolam 5 mg IM

● Haloperidol 5 mg
● Lorazepam 1 mg
● Diphenhydramine 50 mg

● Haloperidol 5 mg IV/IM
● Lorazepam 2 mg IV/IM

items.name: Diazepam IV
information: 5-10 mg IV, repeat every 5-10 minutes as needed

items.name: Lorazepam IV
information: 2-4 mg IV, repeat every 15-20 minutes as needed

items.name: Midazolam
information: ● 2-5 mg IV every 5 minutes x 3 if needed
● increase to 4-10 mg IV if CIWA > 8

items.name: Phenobarbital LOAD IV
information: 5-10 mg/kg IV based on Ideal Body Weight
●  max total dose typically ~ 30 mg/kg Ideal Body Weight

items.name: Phenobarbital
information: ● 130-260 mg IV, repeat every 15-20 minutes as needed
●  increased risk for intubation with used concurrently with benzodiazepines
●  max total dose typically ~ 30 mg/kg Ideal Body Weight

items.name: Propofol
information: Only to be used for intubation procedure or for sedation post-intubation

items.name: Chlordiazepxide
information: 25-100 mg PO if CIWA > 8, can repeat q1 hr as needed

items.name: Epinephrine IM/SubQ 1:1000 (1 mg/mL)
information: ● Max dose 0.5 mg, may repeat every 5-15 mins

items.name: Epi-Pen
information: Most brands contain 0.3 mg/dose, some contain 0.5 mg

items.name: Epinephrine 0.1 mg/mL (1:10,000) IV/IO
information: **Reserve for patients unresponsive to multiple IM injections**
Only consider in impending cardiopulmonary arrest when continuous infusion epinephrine is not immediately available; IV bolus administration may be associated with cardiac arrhythmias.
Initial: 0.05 to 0.1 mg using the 0.1 mg/mL solution (may further dilute in 10 mL of NS for a 0.01 mg/mL (10 mcg/mL) concentration); may repeat every 3 minutes

items.name: Epinephrine Infusion IV/IO
information: ● 0.01-0.2 mcg/kg/min, titrate every 2 to 3 minutes by 0.05 mcg/kg/minute

items.name: Diphenhydramine PO
information: ●  25 mg Q4-6 hours PRN or 50 mg Q6 hours PRN

items.name: Prednisone PO
information: ● 20-60 mg PO Daily, followed by taper over 5-7 days

steps.algostep:
● b/Fondaparinux (Arixtra)/b
reversal requires the 50 units/kg dose

items.name: 4 Factor PCC
information: ● 25-50 units/kg (max 2500 units) for treatment of intracranial hemorrhage
● 50 units/kg (max 5000 units) for other life-threatening hemorrhage)
● Consider rounding dose to whole nearest whole vial

steps.algostep: consider activated charcoal if ingested within 2 hours

b/Protamine:/b
*if < 8 hours since last dose: b/Protamine 50 mg/b
*If 8-12 hours since last dose: b/Protamine 25 mg/b

b/Protamine:/b
*if < 8 hours since last dose: b/Protamine 50 mg IV/b
● b/Vitamin K/b 10 mg IV infuse over 30 minutes

● b/4 Factor PCC (Kcentra):/b
50 units/kg, max 5000 units

● b/Fixed Dose Kcentra:/b
INR < 7.5 and < 100 kg: 1500 units
INR > 7.5 or > 100 kg: 2000 units

● Consider:
b/Vitamin K/b 2.5 mg PO or
b/Vitamin K/b 1-2 mg IV infusion over 30 minutes
● Hold warfarin until INR in therapeutic range

● Consider:
b/Vitamin K/b 2.5 mg PO or
b/Vitamin K/b 1 mg IV infusion over 30 minutes
● Hold warfarin until INR in therapeutic range
● b/Platelets/b: at least 5 units, more may be necessary

items.name: Droperidol IV/IO
information: ● Do not use in patients with known or suspected QT prolongation (QTc >440 msec in males or >450 msec in females) or congenital long QT syndrome
● Use extreme caution in patients >65 years, those with cardiac disease, bradycardia, electrolyte abnormalities, or on medications that prolong QT interval

items.name: Metoclopromide PO
information: 10 mg; may repeat after 4-6 hours if need
comes as 1 mg/mL solution, or 5 and 10 mg tabs, max dose 10 mg

items.name: Ondansetron ODT PO
information: oral dissolving tablet, comes as 4 mg and 8 mg tabs, max dose 8 mg

items.name: Ondansetron IV/IM
information: 4-8 mg IV or 0.15 mg/kg IV Q8-12 hours

items.name: Prochlorperazine IV/IO
information: ● Hypotension is a possibility with IV administration
● Elderly patients should receive dosages in the lower range and be observed closely, as they are more susceptible to hypotension and neuromuscular reactions

items.name: Promethazine  IM/IV
information: IV/IM is generally avoided due to high risk of tissue injury.
●  If used, promethazine should be administered diluted and infused over 10-15 minutes or at a rate not to exceed 25 mg/min.
● Adminster through a large bore vein (not hand or wrist) or via a running IV line at port farthest from patient's vein to reduce risk of phlebitis.

items.name: Promethazine PO
information: This is also the rectal dose. PO also available in 12.5,25, and 50 mg tabs. Max dose 25 mg

items.name: Antimicrobials (IV/IM)
notes: Disclaimer:
● All antimicrobial doses are based on manufacture recommended dosing for their most common indicated uses.
● Confirmation of doses is recommended prior to medication administration.
● Adjustment of dose may be required in the event of renal insufficiency or failure, or with hepatic disease.

items.name: Acyclovir  IV/IO
information: ● HSV (encephalitis or meningitis), herpes zoster (disseminated, opthalmicus), severe varicella, varicella zoster (retinal necrosis, enephalitis)
● 10 mg/kg/dose (based on ideal body weight) IV Q8 hr

items.name: Ampicillin IV/IO/IM
information: ● Streptococcus bacteremia: 1 gram IV Q6-8 hr

dosages.label: ● Late syphillis
dosages.formula: 2.4 million units IM Q week x 3 doses

dosages.label: ● Neurosyphillis
dosages.formula: 2.4 million units IM Q week for 1-2 doses AFTER Pen G IV or Pen G procaine IM

items.name: Vancomycin IV/IO
information: ● Initial dosing presented, consult pharmacy and adhere to policy and institutional guidelines, specific indications, and levels to guide further dosing.

steps.algostep: ● Rate (if patient < 20 kg, add maintenance fluid)
● Rate (if patient < 20 kg, add maintenance fluid)
● Parkland formula plus maintenance fluids, used in patients who weigh < 20 kg
● 3-4 mL lactated ringers * weight x %TBSA burned (second and third degree)
● Half administered over the first 8 hours (time of injury), remaining half administered over the next 16 hours.
* plus 1 mL/kg/hr for each kg more than 20 kg

● > 80 kg:
for every 10 kg > 80 kg, increase rate by 100 mL/hr

items.name: Epinephrine 1 mg/mL (1:1000) Endotracheal
information: ● Follow with saline flush or dilute in 1-5 mL isotonic saline

items.name: Adenosine IV/IO
information: ● Follow immediately with 5-10 mL saline flush

items.name: Amiodarone IV/IO
information: ● ACLS / V Fib or V Tach Arrest: 300 mg
● Stable V Tach or other indications: 150 mg

dosages.label: ● V Tach with pulse, other indications:

items.name: Atropine IV/IO
information: May repeat dose once in 5 minutes. 1.

items.name: Atropine Endotracheal
information: ● Max total dose for adolescents / adults is 3 mg.

items.name: Calcium Gluconate 10% IV/IO
information: ● Substitute for Calcium Chloride if non-emergent situation or no central access.
● In emergencies (cardiac arrest), Calcium Chloride is recommended.

items.name: Diltiazem IV
information: ● 10-20 mg IV or 0.25 mg/kg initial dose over 2 minutes
● Consider lower dose for patients < 70 yo

items.name: Diltiazem IV
information: ● titrate up by 5 mg/hr q15 min to max of 15 mg/hr

items.name: Magnesium Sulfate IV/IO
information: ● Pulseless Rhythm / Torsades: give 1-2 grams over 1-2 minutes
● V-tach with pulse, hypomagnesemia, asthma: give  over 10-20 minutes

items.name: Procainamide IV/IO
information: ● Give over 30-60 minutes as slow IV injection in solution, monitor QT interval.

items.name: Phenylephrine Bolus IV/IO
information: ● Mix 1 mL (10 mg) of Phenylephrine (10 mg/mL) into 100 mL NS

items.name: Atrial Fibrillation / Flutter
notes: ● Initial treatment of these conditions includes control of ventricular rate and restoration of sinus rhythm when applicable

steps.algostep:
● regular, narrow complex; atrial rate usual 250-350
● Ventricular rate typically blocked at 2:1, 3:1, 4:1

items.name: Diltiazem IV
information: ● 10-20 mg IV or 0.25 mg/kg initial dose over 2 minutes
● Consider lower dose for patients < 70 yo

items.name: Diltazem Infusion
information: ● titrate up by 5 mg/hr q15 min to max of 15 mg/hr

items.name: Esmolol IV
information: ● then 0.05 mg/kg/min, may increase by 0.05 mg/kg up to 0.2 mg/kg/min

items.name: Esmolol Infusion
information: ● then 0.05 mg/kg/min, may increase by 0.05 mg/kg up to 0.2 mg/kg/min

items.name: Metoprolol IV
information: ● 2.5-5 mg IV bolus over 2 minutes, can repeat q5 min to total of 15 mg

items.name: Amiodarone Infusion
information: ● 1 mg/min for 6 hours, then decrease to 0.5 mg/min

items.name: DigoxinPO
information: ● can be given IV or PO
PO: Load 250 mcg q2 hr or 10-15 mcg/kg

items.name: Digoxin IV
information: ● 8-12 mcg/kg total loading dose, load 50% initially, then cautiously add 1/4 loading dose Q 6-8 hrs

steps.algostep:
Synchronized Cardioversion
● Atrial Flutter generally requires less energy than Atrial Fibrillation
● anterior-lateral pad positioning has been shown to have higher successful cardioversion with less energy
● No pulse, flat line on monitor
● No pulse, non-shockable rhythm on monitor

● Slow HR (< 60 bpm) with poor perfusion, altered mental status, hypotension, or shock.
● ** Treat only if symptomatic**
● Oxygen and assist breathing as needed

● 1 mg IV Q3-5 minutes (Max 3 mg cumulative)
● Likely ineffective in Mobitz II, 3rd degree AV Block, cardiac transplants
● Consider Epinephrine infusion (see pressure support category)
● Medications, toxins, overdose (consider beta-blockers, digoxin, calcium channel blockers)

items.name: Determine UNSTABLE or STABLE
notes: ● UNSTABLE: hypotension, altered mental status, signs of acute heart failure, cardiac ischemia
● STABLE with Wide QRS
● STABLE with Narrow QRS

steps.algostep: ● If wide complex and no pulse, treat as VENTRICULAR TACHYCARDIA
● Cardioversion at 100 J, defibrillate if irregular or lose pulses
● Consider b/Adenosine/b if rhythm and QRS monomorphic
● Consider synchronized cardioversion if no vascular access or patient becomes UNSTABLE -
● If refractory to b/Adenosine/b or unavailable, consider b/Diltiazem/b if patient is b/STABLE/b
● Refer to b/Atrial Fibrillation/b for dosing
● some recommendations for b/Diltiazem/b as first line therapy in STABLE SVT
● No pulse, fibrillation pattern / no organized electrical complexes
● No pulse, rapid wide QRS complex
● b/CPR/b for 2 minutes, rate 100-120 compressions / minute, then evaluate rhythm / pulse, if VF/VT:
● Continue b/CPR/b for 2 minutes then rhythm / pulse check, if shockable rhythm:
● CPR for 2 minutes then rhythm / pulse check
● b/CPR/b for 2 minutes, rate 100-120 compressions / minute, then evaluate rhythm / pulse, if VF/VT
● Continue CPR for 2 minutes then rhythm / pulse check, if shockable rhythm:
● CPR for 2 minutes, then rhythm / pulse check

items.name: Acetaminophen IV
information: ≥50 kg: 650 mg every 4 hours or 1 g every 6 hours

items.name: Crystalloid BOLUS
information: ● 10-30 mL/kg, septic shock may require larger volumes

items.name: Blood (PRBCs)
information: ● ongoing acute hemorrhage or hemorrhagic shock
● Acute myocardia infarction / unstable angina: < 7.5 g/dL
● Consider for patients with iron deficiency and hemodynamic instability, or for patients with warfarin anticoagulation and severe bleeding or head injury.
● One unit increased hemoglobin by 0.6 to 1.6 g/dL

items.name: Calcium
information: Current recommendation are 1 gram Calcium with first unit of transfused blood, then again after 4 units

items.name: Fresh Frozen Plasma
information: ● replaces clotting factors: II,V, VII, IX, X,  XI,  XII, XIII

items.name: Hypoglycemia
notes: ● Currently defined as: Serum blood glucose < 70 mg/dL (3.9 mmol/L).
● Symptoms may not be present until glucose < 60 mg/dL.

items.name: D10 IV/IO
information: Bolus dose of 100-200 mL IV and / or maintenance infusion of 50-75 mL/hr

items.name: Glucagon IM/IV/SubQ
information: ● May repeat Q15 min x 3 doses, give IV doses over 1-5 minutes
items.brand: Glucagen, Glucagon Emergency Kit, GlucaGen HypoKit, Gvoke

items.name: Preeclampisa
notes: ● clinically defined as hypertension and proteinuria during pregnancy
● Treatment of severe hypertension (SBP>160, DBP>110 mmHg) include the following medications:

items.name: Hydralazine
information: ● Recommended to start at 5 mg over 1-2 minutes, can repeat 5-10 mg in 20 minutes if needed.
● Consider switching to Labetalol if cumulative dose reaches 20-30 mg without desired response

items.name: NIfedipine PO
information: 10 mg PO, can repeat q15-30 minutes up to three times
items.brand: Procardia, Procardia XL, Adalat CC, Nifedical XL, Adalat, Afeditab CR, Nifediac CC

items.name: Eclampisa
notes: ● Pregnancy related hypertension + generalized seizure activity
● Treat seizures initially with Magnesium Sulfate
● Continued Seizures may require benzodiazepines or phenytoin as described in Seizure / Status Epilepticus category
● Treat Hypertension as outlined in Pre-ecmplasia category

items.name: Lorazepam Intranasal
information: ● Midazolam preferred for intranasal administration, use highest concentration available

items.name: Midazolam Intranasal
information: ● Use higher concentration (5 mg/mL) when available

items.name: Oxytocin IV
information: ● rapid administration may result in hyponatremia, hypotension, tachycardia, arrhythmia

items.name: Methylergonovine IM / Intramyometrial
information: ● IM / intramyometrial 0.2 mg every 2-4 hours for maximum 5 doses
● Contraindicated in hypertension and cardiovascular disease

items.name: Methylergonovine PO
information: ● Contraindicated in hypertension and cardiovascular disease

items.name: 15-methyl prostaglandin F2α  IM / Intramyometrial
information: ● 250 μg every 15-90 minutes for maximum 8 doses
● Contraindicated in asthma, cardiovascular, hepatic, and renal disease

items.name: Tranexemic Acid (TXA) IV
information: ● consider when initial medical therapy fails
● Initial 1 gram IV over 10 minutes, repeat if bleeding persists > 30 minutes

items.name: Morphine IV/IO/IM/SubQ
information: 1-4 mg q1-4 hr PRN IV/IO, 5-10 mg q3-4 hr PRN IM

items.name: Fentanyl Intranasal
information: ● 25-50 mcg (1mL max per nostril) ● Adminster 1/2 dose in each nostril, max 1 mL/nostril

items.name: Hydromorphone IV/IO
information: ● Give IV over 2-3 minutes, can also be given IM (not recommended)

dosages.label: ● Pain Managment Q4 hr / PRN dosing

items.name: Ketorolac IV/IM
information: ● Avoid use > 48 hours, avoid in patients with known / high risk for renal insufficiency

items.name: Acetaminophen IV/IO
information: > 50 kg: 650 mg Q4 hrs or 1 gram Q 6 hr

items.name: Oxycodone PO
information: ● 5 mg PO Q4-6 hr PRN, Max 20 mg/day for moderate pain, Max 30 mg/day for severe pain

items.name: Lidocaine IO
information: ● For IO placement, infuse IO line
● Can repeat Q45 minutes, not to exceed 3 mg/kg/day

items.name: Blood Pressure Management
notes: Disclaimer:
● All pressure support medications should preferably be administered via central venous access

steps.algostep:
● Empty 1 mL from 10 mL saline flush
● Draw up 1 mL of Cardiac dose Epinephrine (1 mg/10 mL. 1:10,000)
● Mix 1 mL of Epinephrine with the 9 mL saline
● Concentration is equivalant to 10 mcg/mL
Administer 0.5 -2 mL (5-20 mcg/dose) every 2-5 minutes PRN Hypotension

● If available, use premixed Norepinephrine 4 mg / 250 mL
● To mix:
● add Norepinephrine 4 mg in 4 mL (1 mg/mL) into 250 mL bag of normal saline
● concentration = 4 mg/250 ml (16 mcg/mL)

items.name: Phenylephrine BOLUS
information: ● Draw up 1 mL of phenylephrine (10 mg/mL)
● mix into 100 mL bag of NS

items.name: Phenyephrine Infusion
information: ● Weight Based Infusion: 0.5-3 mcg/kg/min, titrate to clincal endpoint. (Max dose 10 mcg/kg/min - cardiogenic shock)

items.name: Dopamine Infusion
information: ● no utility in low dose rate for renal perfusion
**may have higher mortality in septic shock**

items.name: Esmolol Infusion
information: ● 0.05-0.1 mg/kg/min for 4 min, then repeat loading dose or increase infusion up to 0.3 mg/kg/min as needed

items.name: Labetalol Bolus IV
information: 20 mg IV over 2 minutes, can increase up to 40-80 mg IV Q 10 min

items.name: Milrinone Loading Dose
information: Risk for hypotension, give over 10-60 minutes

items.name: Nicardipene Infusion
information: 5 mg/hr initially, increase by 2.5 mg/hr q5-15 min

items.name: Nitroglycerin (Push-dose)
information: ● Based on SBP, repeat q5 min PRN

items.name: Procedural Sedation
notes: Disclaimer:
● Any patient undergoing procedural sedation should be closely monitored including telemetry, non-invasive blood pressure, pulse oximetry, and end-tidal capnometry

items.name: Propofol IV BOLUS
information: ● repeat 0.5 mg/kg Q 3-5 minutes until adequate level of sedation reached.

items.name: "Ketofol" IV
information: ● most common recommendation is Ketamine 0.5 mg/kg IV followed by Propofol 0.5 mg/kg IV.
● Additional doses of Propfol 0.5 mg/kg can be given every 2-5 minutes as needed for continued sedation.

items.name: Ketamine IV
information: ● May repeat 0.5-1 mg/kg if inadequate sedation in 5-15 minutes

items.name: Ketamine IM
information: ● May repeat 2.5-5 mg/kg if inadequate sedation in 5-10 minutes

items.name: Etomidate IV
information: 0.1-0.2 mg/kg typically used for procedural sedation

items.name: Midazolam IV/IM
information: ● Reduce dose by 30-50% if combined with narcotic

items.name: Methohexital IV
information: ● may repeat at doses of 0.5 mg/kg to max dose of 2 mg/kg

items.name: Reversal Agents
notes: ● Naloxone for Opiates
● Flumazinil for Benzodiazepines

items.name: Naloxone IV/IO/IM/SubQ/Intranasal
information: Titrate dosing to restore respiratory effort

items.name: Asthma / COPD Exacerbation
notes: Supplemental oxygen to maintain oxygen saturation > 92%

items.name: Albuterol SVN
information: ● Can repeat every 20 minutes for 3 doses, then Q1-4 hr PRN

items.name: Albuterol / Ipratropium Bromide
information: 1.5 - 3 mL Q 20 min PRN X 3, then Q 1-4 hours PRN

items.name: Ipratropium Bromide
information: Administer with first three doses of Albuterol.

items.name: Terbutaline SubQ /IM
information: ● Max dose 0.4 mg (0.4 mL of 1 mg/mL solution).
● Can also be given IM, may repeat every 20 minutes x 3 doses
● May give terbutaline OR epinephrine, do NOT give both.

items.name: Terbutaline IV/IO
information: Give initial bolus followed by infusion

items.name: Magnesium Sulfate IV
information: ● Consider IV fluid bolus to minimize risk of associated hypotension

items.name: Epinephrine1 mg/mL (1:1000) IM / SubQ
information: ● Max dose 0.4 mg (0.4 mL of 1 mg/mL solution)

items.name: Lorazepam Intranasal
information: ● Midazolam preferred for intranasal administration, use highest concentration available

items.name: Midazolam Intransal
information: Use higher concentration (5 mg/mL) when available

items.name: Levetiracetam IV Loading Dose
information: ● Use 60 mg/kg for status epilepticus, lower dosing approved for other indications.
items.brand: Keppra, Keppra XR, Spritam, Elepsia XR

items.name: Valproic Acid IV Loading Dose
information: ● Adminster at rate of 1.5-3 mg/kg/min, typically provided in diluted solution
● Dose 20-40 mg/kg, use 40 mg/kg for Status epilepticus

items.name: Fosphenytoin IV Loading Dose
information: ● May repeat with additional 5 PE/kg in 10 min

items.name: Phenytoin IV Loading Dose
information: ● May repeat with additional 5-10 mg/kg in 10 minutes, give at rate of 50 mg/min
● use fosphenytoin in patient in status epilepticus or hemodynamically unstable.

items.name: Phenobarbital IV Loading Dose
information: ● 15-20 mg/kgMay repeat with additional 5-10 mg/kg in 10 minutes.

items.name: Magnesium Sulfate
information: For third trimester of immediate post-partum seizures

items.name: Shock:
notes: Disclaimer:
● All pressure support medications should preferably be administered via central venous access
● All volume concentrations should be confirmed prior to administration

steps.algostep: ● If available, use premixed Norepinephrine 4 mg / 250 mL ● To mix: ● add Norepinephrine 4 mg in 4 mL (1 mg/mL) into 250 mL bag of normal saline ● concentration = 4 mg/250 ml (16 mcg/mL)

items.name: Norepinephrine Infusion
information: ● Weight based Infusion: 0.01-0.4 mcg/kg/min, titrate based on clinical endpoint

steps.algostep:
● Empty 1 mL from 10 mL saline flush
● Draw up 1 mL of Cardiac dose Epinephrine (1 mg/10 mL, 1:10,000)
● Dose is equivalant to 10 mcg/mL
Administer 0.5 -2 mL (5-20 mcg/dose) every 2-5 minutes PRN Hypotension

items.name: Epinephrine Infusion
information: Titrate based on clinical end point

items.name: Phenylephrine Bolus
information: ● Mix 1 mL (10 mg) of Phenylephrine (10 mg/mL) into 100 mL NS

items.name: Phenylephrine Infusion
information: ● Weight Based Infusion: 0.5-3 mcg/kg/min, titrate to clincal endpoint. (Max dose 10 mcg/kg/min - cardiogenic shock)

steps.algostep: ● Theophyine, phenobarbital, digoxin, propoxyphene, nadalol, phenytoin, diazepam, TCAis, chloropropamide, NSADS, salicylates.
● Arsenic, bromide, K+, toxic alcohol, heavy metals

items.name: Cathartics
notes: Use one time with 1st dose charcoal

items.name: Cathartics (Na / MgSo4)
information: use one time with first dose charcoal

steps.algostep: ● Dangerous ingestions < 1 hour old, toxins that slow GI transit, decrease mental status or cause seizure, consider with toxins bound poorly by charcoal
● WARNING: Gastric lavage should rarely, if ever, be performed.
● Caustics (acids, alkalis), solvents, hydrocarbons, nontoxic ingestions

items.name: Polyethylene Glycol
information: Stop when effluent clear or substance removed

steps.algostep: ● Iron, zinc, lithium, sustained release medications, ingested cocaine, or drug packets
● CNS or respiratory depression, ileus, bowel obstruction, perforation

items.name: Acetylcysteine IV
information: ● The loading dose should be infused at 3.75 mL/kg (150 mg/kg) over 15 minutes
** up to 18% of patients develop anaphylactoid reaction**
● Watch for signs of allergic reaction / anaphylaxis.

items.name: Physostigmine
information: ● 0.5-2 mg IV, do not exceed 1 mg/min, repeat q20 minutes as needed.
● If initial dose effective, may give additional 1-4 mg q30-60 minutes

items.name: Toxic Alcohols
notes: ● include methanol and ethylene glycol

items.name: Fomepizole
information: ● Treat until ethylene glycol or methanol levels < 20 mg/dL

items.name: Glucagon IV
information: ● may cause nausea, consider co-adminstration of antiemetic

items.name: Dextrose
information: ● provide 0.5 g/kg dextrose bolus if serum glucose < 400 mg/dL, monitor glucose q20-30 min

items.name: Calcium Channel Blocker Overdose
notes: Notes:
● often present with hypotension and bradycardia, PR prolongation
● consider activated charcoal for stable patients with recent ingestion
● whole-bowel irrigation
● consider IV crystalloid bolus and norepinephrine for hypotension
● Atropine for symptomatic bradycardia
● IV Calcium may cause hyperglycemia

items.name: Calcium Chloride 10%
information: ● Calcium Chloride should be given through central venous access when possible.

items.name: Glucagon Bolus
information: ● 1-5 mg IV push, may repeat up to 15 mg

items.name: Glucagon Infusion
information: ● determine bolus amount necessary to obtain response
● apply "response dose" every hour as infusion

items.name: Insulin Infusion
information: ● titrate upwards until hypotension corrected or max dose 2 units/hr/hr

items.name: Intralipid IV / Infusion
information: ● 1.5 mL/kg IV Bolus, may be repeated x 2 if no response

items.name: Hypoglycemia
notes: ● Currently defined as: Serum blood glucose < 70 mg/dL (3.9 mmol/L).
● Symptoms may not be present until glucose < 60 mg/dL.

items.name: D10 IV
information: D10 is preferred for most patients

items.name: Glucagon IM/IV/SubQ
information: ● May repeat Q15 min x 3 doses.

items.name: Digoxin Immune FAB
notes: Calculated dose: [serum digoxin level (ng/mL) x weight (kg)] / 100 = # vials

items.name: Digoxin Immune FAB
information: ●  1 vial neutralizes estimated 0.5 mg of digoxin
● if known ingestion: total ingested digoxin (mg) / 0.5 = # vials to give

items.name: Naloxone Intranasal
information: ● For Intranasal, use highest concentration available
● Prepared intranasal solutions range from 3 - 8 mg per actuation

items.name: Naloxone Continuous Infusion
information: Alternative dosing: Give 2/3rds of initial effective bolus dose as the hourly infusion.

items.name: Decontamination
notes: ● Aggressive decontamination  including removal of all of patient's clothing and vigorous irrigation of exposed areas.
● Consider activated charcoal for any organophosphate ingestion if within one hour of exposure.

items.name: Atropine
information: Increased and Repeat doses may be necessary

items.name: Pralidoxime IV/IM/SubQ
information: ● 30 mg/kg or 1-2 g over 15-30 minutes, then infuse 4-8 mg/kg/hr or 500 mg/hr
● Infusion may be necessary after initial dose

items.name: Serotonin Syndrome
notes: Hunter Toxicity Criteria Decision Rules:
1. History of recent exposure to a serotonergic drug
2. At least one of the following:
● Spontaneous clonus
● Inducible clonus with agitation or diaphoresis
● Ocular clonus with agitation and diaphoresis
● Tremor and hyperreflexia
● Hypertonia and temperature over 38 °C with ocular or inducible clonus

items.name: Cyproheptadine
information: ● then 2 mg PO q2 hr until improved

steps.algostep: defined as Blood Pressure < 90/60 or MAP < 65-70

items.name: IV Fluids (Crystalloid)
information: ● Agressive isotonic fluid resuscitation has been shown to worsen outcomes in trauma patients

items.name: Hypertonic Saline (3%) IV/IO
information: ● For use in severe traumatic brain injury or herniation, elevated ICP

items.name: Mannitol (20%) IV/IO
information: ● For severe traumatic brain injury, elevated ICP, signs of herniation

dosages.label: ● Repeat dose range based on response / clinical status:

steps.algostep:
● Empty 1 mL from 10 mL saline flush
● Draw up 1 mL of Cardiac dose Epinephrine (1 mg/10 mL, 1:10,000)
● Mix 1 mL of Epinephrine with 9 mL of saline
● Dose is equivalant to 10 mcg/mL
Administer 0.5 -2 mL (5-20 mcg/dose) every 2-5 minutes PRN Hypotension

items.name: Phenylephrine BOLUS IV/IO
information: ● Mix 1 mL (10 mg) of Phenylephrine (10 mg/mL) into 100 mL NS

items.name: Tranexamic Acid(TXA) Infusion IV/IO
information: ● 1000 mg in 100 mL NS over 8 hours

steps.algostep: normal respiratory rate defined as 12-18 Breaths/Minute
defined as Blood Pressure < 90/60 or MAP < 65-70
